name: United States
plain_words: Federal income tax: progressive brackets from 10% to 37%. Standard deduction $14,600 (single) or $29,200 (married). File by April 15. Nine states have no income tax. Sales tax varies by state, 0% to 9.5%+.

taxes.name: Federal Income Tax
taxes.plain_words: Tax on what you earn, in seven brackets from 10% to 37%. Standard deduction $14,600 if single. File by April 15.
taxes.notes: Progressive bracket system. Standard deduction reduces taxable income. 2024 rates.

deadlines.name: filing_deadline
deadlines.detail: File by April 15 (or next business day)

taxes.name: State Income Tax
taxes.plain_words: Some states tax your income, some don't. Nine states have zero income tax: Alaska, Florida, Nevada, New Hampshire, South Dakota, Tennessee, Texas, Washington, Wyoming.
taxes.notes: State rates vary widely. Top 5 highest: CA (13.3%), HI (11%), NJ (10.75%), OR (9.9%), MN (9.85%)

rates.name: no_income_tax_states
rates.notes: AK, FL, NV, NH, SD, TN, TX, WA, WY

taxes.name: Sales Tax
taxes.plain_words: Tax on what you buy. Five states have no sales tax: Alaska, Delaware, Montana, New Hampshire, Oregon. Highest combined rates: Tennessee (9.55%), Arkansas (9.46%), Louisiana (9.45%).
taxes.notes: State + local combined rates vary. 38 states have local sales taxes on top of state rates.
